preview_text:
The model favors Switzerland at 77%, leaving Bosnia and Herzegovina at 4% to win outright. That 73-percentage-point gap reflects a heavily asymmetric matchup in the model's view, with the underdog assigned minimal win probability.

The W/D/L spread widens the picture somewhat. A draw sits at 20% in the model, making it the second-most-likely outcome but still well below a Switzerland victory. The distribution tilts sharply toward the favorite, suggesting low competitive balance; Bosnia and Herzegovina's chances cluster almost entirely in the draw column rather than in outright victory scenarios.

Model and market diverge notably here. The sportsbook consensus prices a Switzerland win at 60%—a 16-percentage-point gap below the model—and assigns Bosnia and Herzegovina 16% rather than 4%. Polymarket consensus aligns closely with the books. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the disagreement may reflect different assumptions about squad strength or match conditions not quantified in the model inputs.
